mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL indicates accelerating inflation expectations, prompting market pricing of tighter future monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during CPIAUCSL uptrends with p-value less than 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in inflation-accelerating periods.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness triggers wage inflation fears and reduces corporate profit margins, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward returns are non-negative when UNRATE falls below 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in low-unemployment environments.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in RSAFS and HOUST signals broad-based consumer and housing demand growth, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Declining INDPRO during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand support, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances, or the regime-conditional predictive accuracy drops below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in PAYEMS reflects expanding employment and rising household income, which directly supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.03, or mean returns are negative during employment expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.0 percent, higher borrowing costs reduce corporate investment and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive when FEDFUNDS crosses 1.0 percent, or the threshold effect shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing with p greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising GDPC1 and falling CPIAUCSL indicates real economic expansion without inflationary pressure, creating favorable conditions for equity markets and positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-CPIAUCSL divergence fails to generate positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in INDPRO during a post-shock recovery phase signal persistent supply chain bottlenecks that delay earnings growth, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in recovery regimes produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 65 percent of cases, or the signal predictive power is indistinguishable from random noise with Rank IC below 0.01.
